_voted_data_note: No 25th Knesset voting data available. Yoaz Hendel (party leader) was not an MK in the 25th Knesset. His earlier voting record from the 23rd Knesset (Blue and White, faction 969) and 24th Knesset aligns with the national_unity data in this dataset. No other party members have Knesset voting records.

stated_position.source: Security-first pragmatism; Hendel is a security hawk but not ideologically pro-settlement
Pragmatic center; no stated position on two-state, focused on security arrangements
Supports military operations with clear strategy and achievable goals
Jerusalem as Israel's capital
Opposes right of return
Opposes annexation; focused on security rather than territorial maximalism
CORE PLATFORM: universal military/national service for all citizens — Haredim, Arabs, and secular alike. Party was founded on this principle by reserve officers
Hendel is secular; supports civil marriage
Supports Shabbat commerce freedom
Supports religious pluralism at Western Wall
Strongly opposes unconditional yeshiva funding — directly tied to party's core conscription stance
Opposes state kashrut enforcement
Center position; supports adequate public healthcare
Supports public housing programs
Supports moderate minimum wage increases
Neutral on privatization
Supports market competition as cost-of-living solution
Hendel as Blue & White MK strongly opposed override clause; consistent position
Opposes political takeover of judiciary appointments
Supports AG authority as rule-of-law safeguard
Supports judicial review including reasonableness standard
Strongly supports judicial independence; part of opposition coalition against judicial reform
Supports equality for all citizens who fulfil civic obligations (service)
Party platform explicitly calls for coalition WITHOUT Arab or Haredi parties — independence from identity-based blocs
Supports equal municipal services; conscription service as path to full civic equality
Neutral on Nation-State Law; not a party focus
Supports PM accountability; part of pro-rule-of-law bloc
Supports term limits for PM
Mid-sized party; would benefit from higher threshold eliminating smaller fringe parties
Supports campaign finance limits
Reserve officers overwhelmingly demand inquiry — it is a core party motivation
Educated, capable workforce essential for military service — core curriculum is a prerequisite
Supports professional media standards and preventing systematic disinformation
